baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: LatentWire transmits tiny source-private packets and evaluates utility per byte; it does not claim raw-accuracy or native serving wins over C2C.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, dense source cache state crosses the interface
baseline_matrix.still_needed: direct native C2C run on matching models/tasks for any stronger claim
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: projected source KV cache fused into target KV cache

baseline_matrix.baseline: Latent Space Communication via K-V Cache Alignment
baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: K/V cache alignment is a direct dense latent-communication competitor; LatentWire must claim only low-rate packet transfer unless direct cache alignment rows are run.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, aligned cache state crosses the boundary
baseline_matrix.still_needed: direct comparison if claiming broad latent communication novelty
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: aligned prefix K/V cache from source model into target model decoding

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: DroidSpeak is cache reuse; LatentWire studies source-private task packets with wrong-row and source-choice controls.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, cache state is reused
baseline_matrix.still_needed: native serving comparison only after GPU setup
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: compatible KV caches across distributed LLM nodes

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: KVCOMM is a high-bandwidth cache communication regime; LatentWire is a low-byte packet protocol with destructive source-private controls.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, cache information crosses the agent boundary
baseline_matrix.still_needed: native baseline if claiming runtime superiority
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: cross-context KV-cache information for multi-agent inference

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: RelayCaching accelerates cache reuse; LatentWire evaluates whether compact source evidence causes target utility without exposing cache/text.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, produced cache state is reused
baseline_matrix.still_needed: native serving baseline for latency or throughput claims
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: decoding-time KV caches for collaborative generation

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: Cache-reuse systems optimize serving reuse for existing context; LatentWire sends small task-level source evidence packets across model boundaries.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, cache or prompt-derived state is reused
baseline_matrix.still_needed: native vLLM/SGLang/LMCache run for throughput claims
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: compressed, streamed, or blended reusable prompt/cache state

baseline_matrix.baseline: Communicating Activations Between Language Model Agents
baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: LatentWire emphasizes source-private low-byte packets, explicit packet bytes, and destructive controls rather than generic activation exchange.
baseline_matrix.source_private: limited; activation vectors are exposed
baseline_matrix.still_needed: cite and distinguish in related-work matrix
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: activation vectors between language-model agents

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: CIPHER pressures broad latent-message novelty; LatentWire's narrower claim is byte-counted source-private packets with destructive controls.
baseline_matrix.source_private: partial; embedding messages are exposed to peers
baseline_matrix.still_needed: avoid claiming generic embedding communication novelty
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: embedding-level messages between debating LLM agents

baseline_matrix.baseline: Direct Semantic Communication via Vector Translation
baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: LatentWire requires low-byte source-private packet accounting and source-choice destructive controls.
baseline_matrix.source_private: partial; dense vectors may expose source state
baseline_matrix.still_needed: verify final citation metadata before camera-ready
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: translated semantic vectors

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: LatentWire claims only packetized, byte-counted, source-private transfer under destructive controls.
baseline_matrix.source_private: partial; dense latent state may cross
baseline_matrix.still_needed: verify final citation metadata before camera-ready
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: intermediate latent representations

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: Prefix tuning adapts one model; LatentWire tests row-specific source evidence packets with wrong-row and source-choice controls.
baseline_matrix.source_private: not a source-to-target communication protocol
baseline_matrix.still_needed: baseline wording in related work
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: task-specific continuous prefix parameters

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: Gist compresses a model's context; LatentWire transfers hidden source evidence between models with source-private controls.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, compression is of visible context
baseline_matrix.still_needed: same-byte visible text baseline remains the local control
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: learned summary tokens for prompt compression

baseline_matrix.included_in_current_eval: same-byte visible text is the local control
baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: LLMLingua compresses visible context; LatentWire tests whether opaque source-private packets add utility beyond same-byte visible text.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, visible prompt content is compressed and exposed
baseline_matrix.still_needed: optional stronger visible-text compression baseline for ICLR
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: compressed visible prompt text or retained prompt tokens

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: LatentWire's novelty is not query bottlenecks; it is packetized source-private model-to-model transfer plus destructive controls.
baseline_matrix.source_private: not a source-to-target LLM communication protocol
baseline_matrix.still_needed: cite as precedent, not baseline claim
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: query bottleneck between vision encoder and language model

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: LatentWire differs by source-private packets, byte accounting, and controls against source-choice artifacts.
baseline_matrix.source_private: not source-private model-to-model communication
baseline_matrix.still_needed: cite as precedent, not novelty
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: fixed latent resampler tokens into LM cross-attention

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: Latent arrays are prior art; LatentWire's claim is communication protocol evaluation under strict controls.
baseline_matrix.source_private: not a source-to-target LLM communication protocol
baseline_matrix.still_needed: cite as motivation only
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: latent query array for generic input/output mapping

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: Common bases alone are not novel; LatentWire must show sparse features cause source-private downstream utility under atom-shuffle and wrong-row controls.
baseline_matrix.source_private: feature analysis, not packet transfer
baseline_matrix.still_needed: future SRP method should compare directly if using crosscoders
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: shared and private features across activation spaces

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: LatentWire uses common-basis work only as a possible packet representation; novelty requires byte-counted causal packet utility.
baseline_matrix.source_private: feature analysis, not packet transfer
baseline_matrix.still_needed: future SRP baseline if PCA succeeds
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: cross-model sparse concept basis

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: Transcoders can define interpretable atoms, but LatentWire must prove that packetized atoms causally improve target behavior under strict controls.
baseline_matrix.source_private: analysis method, not direct packet transfer
baseline_matrix.still_needed: strong future baseline for behavior-transcoder packet branch
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: sparse feature-to-feature computation approximating model internals

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: SAE quality and feature stability are prerequisites if Sparse Resonance Packets use sparse atoms; they are not themselves the communication result.
baseline_matrix.source_private: analysis/evaluation method
baseline_matrix.still_needed: use if upgrading from PCA/SVD to SAE/crosscoder packets
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: benchmarking and reliability checks for sparse feature dictionaries

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: TurboQuant reduces dense state cost; LatentWire sends task-level packets and must avoid unmeasured throughput claims.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, quantized source state still exposes state
baseline_matrix.still_needed: native comparison after NVIDIA hardware
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: low-bit quantized vectors/KV state

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: KVQuant compresses one model's cache; LatentWire transfers compact source evidence across models.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, cache is still exposed/reused
baseline_matrix.still_needed: native comparison after NVIDIA hardware
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: low-bit KV cache for long-context serving

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: These methods shrink dense state; LatentWire transfers task-level packets and should compare byte floors without claiming native speed.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, dense state is compressed rather than hidden
baseline_matrix.still_needed: native low-bit cache comparison for ICLR systems claims
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: low-bit KV/cache values or sketch-corrected quantized vectors

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: H2O reduces one model's cache memory; LatentWire sends source-private cross-model evidence packets.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, same-model cache retention
baseline_matrix.still_needed: long-context reviewer baseline, not direct COLM_v2 competitor
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: retained heavy-hitter KV cache tokens

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: SnapKV is same-model long-context compression; LatentWire is source-to-target packet communication.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, same-model cache compression
baseline_matrix.still_needed: systems appendix comparator if adding long-context tasks
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: selected KV cache tokens before generation

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: Quest optimizes attention cache loading; LatentWire optimizes byte-limited model-to-model evidence transfer.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, same-model cache sparsity
baseline_matrix.still_needed: native systems appendix if reviewers demand long-context baselines
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: query-aware sparse KV pages for long-context inference

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: KVzip is cache storage compression; LatentWire is packetized cross-model communication with source-private controls.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, same-model cache compression
baseline_matrix.still_needed: appendix context if broad systems framing expands
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: query-agnostic compressed KV cache with reconstruction objective

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: vLLM is a required runtime baseline for TTFT/TPOT/goodput/HBM once GPU measurements are available.
baseline_matrix.source_private: not a communication method
baseline_matrix.still_needed: native NVIDIA run before any latency/HBM claim
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: paged KV-cache serving substrate

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: SGLang is a second serving substrate for native claims; Mac byte accounting cannot replace it.
baseline_matrix.source_private: not a communication method
baseline_matrix.still_needed: native NVIDIA run before any latency/HBM claim
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: structured LM serving runtime with KV reuse

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: LatentWire must beat this to show more than answer-choice copying.
baseline_matrix.source_private: yes but artifact-prone
baseline_matrix.still_needed: keep in all packet tables
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: the source's preferred answer index or label

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: LatentWire must exceed score/rank packet controls to claim richer communication.
baseline_matrix.source_private: yes but may reveal source choice behavior
baseline_matrix.still_needed: include in claim audit
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: source ranking or quantized score vector

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: LatentWire packets must justify why opaque source-private bytes are useful relative to visible text at the same byte budget.
baseline_matrix.source_private: no, visible text crosses the boundary
baseline_matrix.still_needed: include in strict-control table
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: human-readable text within the same byte budget

baseline_matrix.latentwire_distinction: Passing these controls is the main evidence that the packet is row-specific source communication rather than receiver artifact.
baseline_matrix.source_private: yes
baseline_matrix.still_needed: keep as hard gate for ICLR
baseline_matrix.what_transfers: noncausal or target-only packet with matched format

benchmark_breadth.caveat: headline-safe only when paired with source-index and source-choice boundary wording
headline-safe only when paired with source-index and source-choice boundary wording
passes validation1024 seed gate; full-validation terminal tail blocks benchmark-complete claim
source-private split/control contract is frozen, but no positive packet row exists yet

claim_audit.claim: LatentWire defines a source-private candidate-transfer packet protocol and strict evaluation framework.
claim_audit.controls_passed: source-private interface, wrong-row/source-choice controls where available
claim_audit.evidence_artifact: COLM_v2 review packet plus COLM_v3 review packet

claim_audit.claim: Low-byte packets show narrow same-family utility on ARC-style rows.
claim_audit.controls_passed: target-only and same-byte/text controls on the reported rows; source-index remains a hard boundary
claim_audit.required_wording: narrow source-private candidate-transfer utility, not broad latent communication

claim_audit.claim: The current packet beats source-index communication or selected-candidate codes.
claim_audit.controls_passed: packet-source lower bounds remain negative or zero
claim_audit.required_wording: do not claim; source-index is the main boundary

claim_audit.claim: Many apparent wins collapse into source-choice, source-rank, or target-cache artifacts.
claim_audit.controls_passed: same-source-choice wrong-row, source-index/rank/score, and destructive controls where available
claim_audit.required_wording: use as a reviewer-strengthening result, not as the headline alone

claim_audit.claim: LatentWire beats C2C or dense KV/cache transfer.
claim_audit.controls_passed: none; no native matched C2C row
claim_audit.required_wording: do not claim; compare as byte/exposure boundary only

claim_audit.claim: LatentWire has native GPU latency, HBM, energy, or throughput wins.
claim_audit.required_wording: future work until native measurements exist

claim_audit.claim: LatentWire solves broad latent model-to-model communication or cross-family transfer.
claim_audit.controls_passed: cross-family falsification weakened the broad claim
claim_audit.required_wording: do not claim; present as an open ICLR goal

contribution_table.evidence: packet rows and source-private interface definition
contribution_table.still_needs_work: paper prose must avoid broad latent-language claims
compress into one main table plus appendix
state that source-index remains a strong boundary
no native GPU/HBM/energy claim until NVIDIA runbook is executed
keep as ICLR future method target

experiment_scoping.colm_v3_scope: separate systems spinout; exclude from COLM_v3 claims unless Phase 1 confirms novelty, Phase 2 shows at least 3% theoretical benefit, and native GPU profiling confirms overhead
experiment_scoping.highest_value_gate: vLLM hybrid SSM/disaggregated serving source audit
experiment_scoping.novelty_risk: boundary fusion may already be covered by vLLM/vendor hybrid serving optimizations

experiment_scoping.colm_v3_scope: separate systems spinout only; mention in COLM_v3 only as future work unless Phase 1-4 produce source-backed novelty plus a reference artifact
experiment_scoping.novelty_risk: learned/per-head sink denominator handling is already occupied by FlashInfer/FlashMLA/GPT-OSS-style paths

experiment_scoping.colm_v3_scope: separate systems spinout candidate after Phase 1, not current COLM_v3 evidence
experiment_scoping.highest_value_gate: Mac-only trace simulation for protected reasoning-token classes
experiment_scoping.novelty_risk: field is crowded by LongFlow, ThinKV, R-KV/R-KVHash, RaaS, LazyEviction, ForesightKV, and PM-KVQ

main_claim: LatentWire provides a practical protocol and evaluation framework for source-private candidate-transfer packets, with controlled evidence of narrow fixed-byte packet utility, explicit utility-per-byte accounting, and destructive controls that expose shortcut claims.

main_results.decision: Use as the COLM_v2 positive method boundary; not enough for ICLR generality.
main_results.evidence: 16/16 disjoint n500 rows pass; 4/4 budget-2 rows pass; cross-family grid is 0/28.

main_results.decision: Useful COLM_v2 systems/privacy row, but not a learned latent receiver.
main_results.evidence: Full cached validation pass over 10042 rows; positive on 10/10 slices.

main_results.decision: Do not continue scalar threshold integrity on this public-zscore conditional-PQ receiver.
main_results.evidence: Selected negative_min_l2 threshold; source 0.425781 vs best control label_shuffled_encoder at 0.457031; source/max-corrupt accept 0.773438/1.000000.

main_results.decision: Do not promote as a positive second-benchmark row; the same-source-choice wrong-row control nearly matches the receiver, so it is a source-choice artifact diagnostic.
main_results.evidence: Held-out test n=500; receiver candidate pass False; default receiver 0.424000 vs best baseline/control 0.422000; aggregate seed-row CI low 0.000400; strict per-seed CI 2/5.

main_results.decision: Use only as capacity/headroom evidence; it optimizes on eval rows.
main_results.evidence: 3/3 tiny oracle rows pass; best optimized agreement 0.937500 beats shuffled_optimized_prefix at 0.625000.

main_results.decision: Do not train another HellaSwag selector on the same packet fields; require a new information path.
main_results.evidence: Fixed+source top1/top2 oracle 0.694010; source top1/top2 covers 174 fixed-hybrid errors, but selected frontier makes 0 overrides.

main_results.decision: Do not continue cached Qwen policy-prediction packets on this HellaSwag slice.
main_results.evidence: Selector accuracy 0.455729 vs fixed 0.467448; overrides 30 rows; best destructive control field_shuffle_multisignal_control at 0.430990.

negative_results.decision: Do not widen public-zscore to n500/remap.

negative_results.branch: Conditional PQ public-SVD whitening held-out-family decoder
negative_results.decision: Do not continue deterministic public-basis whitening as the rescue path.
negative_results.evidence: 0/2 pass; best source-minus-control -0.238281 against permuted_codes. Worst row -0.500000.

negative_results.decision: Keep as diagnostic; do not promote this receiver family.
Do not continue scalar threshold integrity on this public-zscore conditional-PQ receiver.
negative_results.evidence: Selected negative_min_l2 threshold; source 0.425781 vs best control label_shuffled_encoder at 0.457031; source/max-corrupt accept 0.773438/1.000000.

negative_results.branch: ARC sparse resonance PCA/target-aligned soft-prefix packets
negative_results.decision: Do not widen plain PCA/target-aligned soft-prefix packets.
negative_results.evidence: 0/4 pass; best matched-minus-control -0.375000 with best control source_free_prefix.

negative_results.decision: Keep the strict atom harness, but do not scale the current behavior/PCA/BatchTopK-style atom basis without a new source-causality mechanism.
negative_results.evidence: 0/5 pass; best matched 0.437500 vs required control top_atom_knockout at 0.437500; fired 8 rows, helps/harms 3/0.

negative_results.decision: Do not promote as a positive second-benchmark row; the same-source-choice wrong-row control nearly matches the receiver, so it is a source-choice artifact diagnostic.
negative_results.evidence: Held-out test n=500; receiver candidate pass False; default receiver 0.424000 vs best baseline/control 0.422000; aggregate seed-row CI low 0.000400; strict per-seed CI 2/5.

negative_results.decision: Do not rerun generic protected top-2/rival switchers.
negative_results.evidence: Oracle 0.678385, selected decoder harms 6 vs helps 2.

negative_results.decision: Do not continue shallow linear score-feature selectors without new evidence.
negative_results.evidence: Oracle 0.766927; eval-label diagnostic delta is only 0.002604.
Selected scheme no_op with 0 eligible buckets and 0 overrides.

negative_results.decision: Do not continue rank/score-bin top2 buckets without a new packet field.
negative_results.evidence: Source top1/top2 oracle 0.675781, but selected bucket overrides 0 rows.

negative_results.decision: Do not promote ridge-denoising syndrome decoder.
negative_results.evidence: Target/hybrid oracle 0.604167; denoising helps 2 and harms 5.

negative_results.decision: Do not claim sparse atom causality from this branch.
negative_results.evidence: Best destructive control target_derived_source_pair_ambiguity_control at 0.501953; atom code helps 0 and harms 1.

negative_results.decision: Use only as capacity/headroom evidence; it optimizes on eval rows.
negative_results.evidence: 3/3 tiny oracle rows pass; best optimized agreement 0.937500 beats shuffled_optimized_prefix at 0.625000.

negative_results.branch: Target self-resonance held-out learned prefix encoders
negative_results.decision: Do not run more chunk/distill/query-resampler variants without a new information path.
negative_results.evidence: 1/5 pass; best agreement delta 0.000000 against slots_only_encoder; worst agreement delta -0.125000.

negative_results.decision: Diagnose complementarity/gating before implementing another source-to-prefix decoder.
negative_results.evidence: 0/5 pass; best accuracy delta 0.000000 against zero_source_hidden; source top1/top2 oracle reaches 1.000000.

negative_results.decision: Do not train another HellaSwag selector on the same packet fields; require a new information path.
negative_results.evidence: Fixed+source top1/top2 oracle 0.694010; source top1/top2 covers 174 fixed-hybrid errors, but selected frontier makes 0 overrides.

negative_results.decision: Do not continue cached Qwen policy-prediction packets on this HellaSwag slice.
negative_results.evidence: Selector accuracy 0.455729 vs fixed 0.467448; overrides 30 rows; best destructive control field_shuffle_multisignal_control at 0.430990.

next_exact_gate: human copyedit, page-budget review, final PDF/table placement, and consistency check between paper, review packet, and artifact manifest

readiness.iclr: still blocked by lack of broad source-causal positive method
readiness.workshop_blocker: human copyedit, page-budget review, and final PDF/table placement; no new speculative experiment is required unless review exposes a missing claim-supporting row

story: LatentWire studies whether compact source-private candidate packets can transmit useful model evidence without dense cache transfer, and uses byte accounting plus destructive controls to separate real packet utility from answer-choice and target-cache shortcuts.

submission_checklist.blocker: requires human copyedit and page-budget review
submission_checklist.item: Main claim agrees across abstract, intro, results, limitations.
Every table and figure maps to a claim in the claim audit.
Systems claims separate measured packet bytes from analytical KV/cache floors.

submission_checklist.blocker: page-budget review may require moving matrix to appendix
submission_checklist.item: Related work distinguishes dense KV/cache transfer, compression, and packet controls.
Limitations explicitly cover source-choice artifacts and cross-family failures.

submission_checklist.blocker: remaining panel risks are claim-boundary risks, not missing paper sections
submission_checklist.item: Ten-reviewer stress panel is recorded and actioned.
Experimental side projects are scoped away from COLM_v3 claims.

systems_checks.value: native NVIDIA rows are pending and non-claims

systems_headline.claim_scope: Paper-ready systems boundary artifact: LatentWire cached-source rows count the fixed-byte source-private packet object; paired end-to-end rows disclose source scoring separately. KV/cache rows are byte floors or pending native serving baselines. The artifact supports byte/exposure accounting, not a native C2C/KVComm/TurboQuant/QJL/vLLM/SGLang win.

systems_measured_vs_estimated.claim_allowed: sub-4-bit KV comparator only
systems_measured_vs_estimated.overclaim_guard: KVQuant is a KV-cache quantization comparator; do not claim it is a packet baseline.

systems_measured_vs_estimated.claim_allowed: KV/vector quantization comparator only
systems_measured_vs_estimated.overclaim_guard: TurboQuant is a vector/KV quantizer; byte floors alone do not compare native latency.

systems_measured_vs_estimated.claim_allowed: selective-KV communication boundary only
systems_measured_vs_estimated.overclaim_guard: KVComm shares selected KV pairs; this is only a native-pending 30%-layer byte floor.

systems_measured_vs_estimated.claim_allowed: closest cache-transfer baseline; native run still required
systems_measured_vs_estimated.overclaim_guard: C2C fuses source KV cache; do not claim superiority without native C2C.

systems_measured_vs_estimated.claim_allowed: systems neighbor only; native run still required
systems_measured_vs_estimated.overclaim_guard: KVCOMM is a cross-context KV reuse system; this row is only a native-pending byte floor.

systems_measured_vs_estimated.claim_allowed: native TTFT/TPOT/goodput/HBM target, not closed on Mac
systems_measured_vs_estimated.overclaim_guard: No vLLM serving claim is valid until native NVIDIA rows are measured.

systems_measured_vs_estimated.claim_allowed: native TTFT/TPOT/goodput/HBM target, not closed on Mac
systems_measured_vs_estimated.overclaim_guard: No SGLang serving claim is valid until native NVIDIA rows are measured.
